name: Orwell Route Trail
name_original: Trincheras de la Guerra Civil y Canal de Monegros — gravel circular desde Alcubierre
description: This 26.5-mile gravel circular route from Alcubierre takes you through Civil War trenches and along the Canal de Monegros in the Sierra de A
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alcubierre, located in the province of Huesca, Spain, offers a diverse landscape for cyclists. The region is characterized by the Sierra de Alcubierre mountain range, featuring pine forests and varied elevations. It also encompasses parts of the arid Monegros Desert, providing open spaces and unique terrain. Views of the Ésera and Cinca Valleys add to the scenic routes available. Best touring cycling routes around Alcubierre The most popular touring cycling route is Orwell Route Trail, a 26.5 miles (42.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 24 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the Monegros region and its historical sites. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Cartuja de Monegros and Sariñena Lagoon Loop, a moderate 22.4 miles (36.1 km) path. This route loops through the Monegros region and includes the Sariñena Lagoon. Local touring cyclists also love the Camino Real de Zaragoza and Sierra de Alcubierre Loop, a 28.3 miles (45.6 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Alcubierre, often completed in about 4 hours 20 minutes. Touring cycling around Alcubierre is defined by the Sierra de Alcubierre mountain range, the Monegros Desert, and views of the Ésera and Cinca Valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcubierre's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alcubierre?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;12 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Alcubierre listed on komoot, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists. These routes range from moderate to difficult, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Alcubierre?
A: The terrain around Alcubierre is quite diverse. You'll encounter the pine forests and varied elevations of the &lt;strong&gt;Sierra de Alcubierre&lt;/strong&gt;, as well as the unique arid steppe landscape of the &lt;strong&gt;Monegros Desert&lt;/strong&gt; with its vast open spaces. Some routes also offer scenic views of the Ésera and Cinca Valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes in the Alcubierre region?
A: Yes, Alcubierre offers several challenging routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379787192/camino-real-de-zaragoza-and-sierra-de-alcubierre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Real de Zaragoza and Sierra de Alcubierre Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.6 km trail that takes about 4 hours 20 minutes to complete. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bujal Viewpoint – Farlete Bell Tower and Murals loop from Farlete&lt;/a&gt;, covering 49.2 km with significant elevation gain.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while cycling in Alcubierre?
A: The region is rich in history. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974058980/orwell-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell Route Trail&lt;/a&gt; allows you to trace George Orwell's path through the Monegros and visit reconstructed fortifications from the Spanish Civil War. You can also visit &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5771305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Posición San Simón&lt;/a&gt;, an old Civil War defensive position with preserved trenches and lookout posts.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alcubierre?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379795104/cartuja-de-monegros-and-sarinena-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartuja de Monegros and Sariñena Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the Monegros region, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379787192/camino-real-de-zaragoza-and-sierra-de-alcubierre-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Real de Zaragoza and Sierra de Alcubierre Loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering a journey through the mountain range.

Q: What are some notable natural landmarks or viewpoints along the cycling routes?
A: Cyclists can enjoy several natural landmarks and viewpoints. The ascent to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300059" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Oscuro Weather Radar&lt;/a&gt; offers breathtaking panoramic views of the Cinca Medio, the Pyrenees, and the Monegros. You can also find stunning vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5753138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enebro Peak&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5285101" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Summit of Monte Oscuro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of attractions can I find near the touring cycling routes in Alcubierre?
A: Beyond the routes themselves, you can explore unique attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Caprasio Caves&lt;/a&gt;, which have historical associations with hermits and shepherds. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5300070" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of the Sabina&lt;/a&gt;, surrounded by pine and juniper trees, also offers a peaceful stop with views of the Cinca River.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Alcubierre?
A: Given the arid steppe climate of the Monegros, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Alcubierre. Temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is often at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, and winters can be chilly, so plan accordingly.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by lakes or lagoons?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379795104/cartuja-de-monegros-and-sarinena-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartuja de Monegros and Sariñena Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate route that specifically includes the Sariñena Lagoon, offering a chance to experience this natural water feature within the Monegros region.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Alcubierre?
A: Over 20 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alcubierre's varied terrain. They often praise the unique blend of the Sierra de Alcubierre's pine forests and the expansive, austere beauty of the Monegros Desert, as well as the historical insights offered by routes like the Orwell Trail.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for beginners or those looking for a moderate ride?
A: Yes, there are moderate options available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974058980/orwell-route-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orwell Route Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 42.6 km route that explores the Monegros region and its historical sites, taking about 3 hours 24 minutes. Another moderate choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1379795104/cartuja-de-monegros-and-sarinena-lagoon-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cartuja de Monegros and Sariñena Lagoon Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 36.1 km long.
tips: On the A-129 road from Leciñena to Alcubierre (km 34.6) take the detour that leads to the position of Monte Irazo, recreated by the writer. The Orwell Route will allow you to explore the Monegrino landscapes among the sandbags, machine gun posts and military redoubts, which have been recreated as they were during the war. From the trenches where Orwell was, you can see the towers of the Pilar de Zaragoza, since that front line aspired to conquer the city.
Trenches that were used in the Spanish Civil War. This area was one of the areas where the armed battles were tougher and more lasting.

Monegros Canal near the town of Robres.
The Monegros canal is a canal designed in the Alto Aragón Irrigation Plan of 1902. Its construction began in 1915 and it is 130 kilometers long.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this gravel circular route?
faqs.answer: This 42.6 km gravel circular route is categorized as a moderate bike ride. It requires good fitness due to its length and an elevation gain of around 410 meters. While it's a gravel route, some sections may be challenging due to unpaved surfaces.

faqs.question: What type of bike is recommended for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the gravel terrain and moderate difficulty, a gravel bike or a mountain bike is highly recommended. A road bike would not be suitable for the unpaved sections.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The route is a circular from Alcubierre. While specific parking details aren't provided, Alcubierre serves as the natural starting point for this loop. It's advisable to check local maps or resources for designated parking areas within Alcubierre.

faqs.question: What historical sites and natural features can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: This route is rich in both history and unique natural beauty. You'll pass by significant Spanish Civil War sites, including the restored trenches in the Sierra de Alcubierre, such as Las Tres Huegas - Civil War Trenches. You'll also encounter Orwell Hill, named after George Orwell who served here. The route also follows the Canal de Monegros, offering varied scenery through the distinctive semi-desert landscape of the Monegros region.

faqs.question: What makes the Monegros region unique on this trail?
faqs.answer: The Monegros region, often described as a semi-desert or steppe, offers a truly unique visual experience. It's recognized for its exceptional biological value, with a high degree of endemism and over 5,400 documented biological species, making it a unique natural area in Europe. The Sierra de Alcubierre, with its pine and juniper forests, also adds to the diverse landscape.

faqs.question: When is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Monegros region can experience extreme temperatures. Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally the best times to cycle this route, offering more moderate temperatures and pleasant conditions for exploring both the historical sites and the unique landscape.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the Trenches of the Civil War and Canal de Monegros route?
faqs.answer: While there are no explicit restrictions mentioned, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near historical sites. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or is there an entrance fee for the Sierra de Alcubierre area?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sierra de Alcubierre or to cycle this route. The historical sites, like the trenches, are generally open for public visitation as part of cultural tourism initiatives.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or water stops along the route?
faqs.answer: The route traverses a semi-desert region, and amenities can be sparse outside of Alcubierre. It is highly recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks for the entire 3-4 hour duration of the ride. Plan to refuel in Alcubierre before and after your tour.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter in the Monegros region?
faqs.answer: The Monegros region is known for its diverse fauna, particularly birds of prey. Keep an eye out for various raptor species soaring above the unique semi-desert landscape. The area's high biodiversity means you might also spot other endemic species.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a distinct circular route, sections of this trail overlap with other paths. Approximately 8% of the route follows parts of the Sierra de Alcubierre trail, and about 7% intersects with different segments of the Camino Loma Orwell.

waypoints.name: Las Tres Huegas - Civil War Trenches
